steps.title: Solve the unknown directly
steps.content: For no solution, the $x$-coefficients must match while the constants differ. Matching the $x$-coefficients gives $r=\frac{5}{9}$.

steps.title: Confirm algebraically
steps.content: Start with $$-\frac{3}{19}rx+\frac{s}{8}=10-\frac{5}{57}x.$$ Match the $x$-coefficients: $$-\frac{3}{19}r=-\frac{5}{57}.$$ Multiply both sides by $-1$ and solve: $$\frac{3}{19}r=\frac{5}{57}$$ $$r=\frac{5}{57}\cdot\frac{19}{3}=\frac{95}{171}=\frac{5}{9}.$$

steps.title: Why this gives no solution
steps.content: With $r=\frac{5}{9}$, the $x$-terms are identical on both sides. If the constants are different, the equation reduces to a contradiction like $\frac{s}{8}=10$. The question only asks for the $r$ that makes no solution possible, so the answer is $\frac{5}{9}$.
